note: Diagnostic question review queue. 34 original live questions (reviewed+approved, source=original-live) plus 77 new drafts (pending). Only approved questions are served to the live diagnostic. Answer-key fixes vs original draft: L5-A-004 (0->1), L5-A-005 (1->4), L7-S-002 (0.52->0.53).

L3-N-001.text: What is the value of the digit 6 in 36,251?
L3-N-001.explanation: The 6 is in the thousands column. 36,251 = 30,000 + 6,000 + 200 + 50 + 1.

L3-N-002.explanation: Tens digit is 4 (less than 5) so round down. Answer: 7,800.

L3-G-001.text: Angles on a straight line add to 180°. One angle is 65°. What is the other angle?

L4-N-002.text: Calculate 2/5 + 3/10. The answer is ?/10. Type the numerator only.

L4-A-001.text: If n = 5, what is the value of 3n + 4?

L4-G-001.text: A triangle has angles 65° and 48°. What is the third angle?

L5-N-001.text: A shop has 120 books. 35% are science books. How many science books?

L5-N-002.text: Share £240 in ratio 3:5. How much is the larger share?

L5-A-002.text: nth term of a sequence is 4n − 3. What is the 25th term?

L5-G-001.text: Area of a triangle with base 10 cm and perpendicular height 6 cm.

L5-S-001.text: Mean of 5 numbers is 12. Four are 8, 14, 10, 15. What is the fifth?

L6-N-001.text: Price rises from £80 to £96. What is the percentage increase?
L6-N-001.explanation: Change = 16. % = (16 ÷ 80) × 100 = 20%. Common error: dividing by 100 gives 16%.

L6-N-002.text: What is the HCF of 24 and 36?

L6-A-001.text: Expand and simplify: 3(2x + 5) − 2(x − 3). Answer is ax + b. What is a?

L6-G-001.text: Right triangle with shorter sides 5 cm and 12 cm. Find the hypotenuse.

L6-S-001.text: Bag has 4 red and 6 blue balls. P(red)? Type as a decimal.

L7-N-001.text: 0.000045 = 4.5 × 10ⁿ. What is the value of n?

L7-G-001.text: Area of circle with radius 5 cm. Use π = 3.14. Answer to 1 decimal place.

L7-S-001.text: P(A) = 0.4, P(B) = 0.3, independent. What is P(A and B)?

L8-A-001.text: Solve: 2x + y = 11 and x − y = 1. What is x?

L8-A-002.text: x² + 7x + 12 = 0. Which pair satisfies this equation?
L8-A-002.explanation: (x+3)(x+4)=0. x=−3 or x=−4. Common error: forgetting the sign change.

L8-N-001.text: √50 = a√2. What is the value of a?

L8-G-001.text:
Right triangle: opposite = 5, hypotenuse = 13. sin θ = 5/13.
Find θ in degrees (1 decimal place). Calculator allowed.

L8-S-001.text:
Bag: 4 red, 6 blue. Draw 2 WITHOUT replacement.
P(both red) = ?/15. Type the numerator only.

L9-A-001.text: Solve x² − 5x + 6 = 0. What is the larger solution?
L9-A-001.inputHint: Type the larger value of x

L9-N-001.explanation: 27^(1/3) = 3. Then 3² = 9. Common error: stopping at cube root gives 3.

L9-G-001.text:
O is centre. Angle AOB = 80° (at centre).
Angle ACB where C is on the major arc = ?
L9-G-001.explanation: Angle at centre = 2 × circumference angle. ACB = 80÷2 = 40°.

L9-S-001.text:
Bag: 3 red, 7 blue. Draw 2 without replacement.
P(both same colour) as decimal to 2dp.

L3-N-003.text: Write the number five thousand and forty-two in digits.

L4-N-003.text: Write 0.7 as a fraction in its simplest form. The denominator is 10. Type the numerator.
L4-N-003.explanation: 0.7 = 7/10. Already in simplest form as 7 and 10 share no common factors.
L4-N-003.distractor_notes: Common error: writing 7/100 (confusing tenths with hundredths)

L4-N-004.distractor_notes: Common error: 4 (only counting proper factors, missing 1 and 12)

L4-N-005.text: Simplify the fraction 18/24. Type the numerator of the simplified fraction.
L4-N-005.distractor_notes: Common error: dividing by 2 only to get 9/12, not fully simplified

L5-N-004.text: A recipe needs flour and sugar in ratio 5:2. If you use 350g of flour, how many grams of sugar?

L5-N-005.text: A shirt costs £40. It is reduced by 20% in a sale. What is the sale price?
L5-N-005.inputHint: Type the sale price in pounds
L5-N-005.explanation: 20% of 40 = £8. Sale price = 40 − 8 = £32. Or multiply by 0.80.

L6-N-003.text: A car was worth £12,000. It is now worth £9,600. What is the percentage decrease?
L6-N-003.distractor_notes: 24%: divided by 9600 (new value). 2400%: forgot to multiply by 100. 80%: calculated what remains.

L6-N-004.explanation: 3² × 3³ = 3^(2+3) = 3^5 = 243. When multiplying same base, add powers.
L6-N-004.distractor_notes: Common error: 9 (multiplied 2×3 instead of adding powers), 3^6=729

L6-N-005.text: Express 60 as a product of prime factors. How many prime factors are there (counting repeats)?
L6-N-005.inputHint: Type the count of prime factors
L6-N-005.distractor_notes: Common error: 3 (not counting both 2s), 5 (counting factors of 60 instead)

L7-N-003.text: £500 is invested at 4% compound interest per year. How much after 2 years? Round to nearest penny.
L7-N-003.distractor_notes: Common error: £540 (used simple interest), £541.60 (added 4% of 500 twice)

L8-N-002.text: Simplify √75. Answer is a√3. What is a?

L9-N-002.text: After a 25% increase, a price is £150. What was the original price?
L9-N-002.inputHint: Type the original price in pounds

L9-N-003.text: Rationalise the denominator of 6/√3. Answer is a√3. What is a?
L9-N-003.distractor_notes: Common error: a=6 (forgot to simplify), a=√2 (wrong rationalisation)

L3-A-002.text: The pattern is: 2, 5, 8, 11, ... What is the 10th term?
L3-A-002.distractor_notes: Common error: 30 (forgot first term is 2, not 3), 32 (10×3+2)

L3-A-003.text: If ★ + ★ + ★ = 18, what is the value of ★ + 5?

L4-A-002.text: If x = 3, find the value of x² + 2x − 1.

L5-A-003.distractor_notes: Common error: x=8.33 (divided 20 by 3 first), x=25 (multiplied)

L5-A-004.text: A sequence has nth term 3n + 2. Is 50 in the sequence? Type 1 for yes, 0 for no.
L5-A-004.explanation: 3n+2=50. 3n=48. n=16. Yes, 50 IS in the sequence. 3(16)+2=50. Answer is 1.
L5-A-004.distractor_notes: Answer-key fix applied: was 0, corrected to 1 to match explanation.

L5-A-005.distractor_notes: Answer-key fix applied: was 1, corrected to 4 to match explanation. Common error: x=5.5 (divided 14 by 2 first, forgot to subtract 3).

L6-A-002.text: Expand: (x + 4)(x + 3). The answer is x² + bx + 12. What is b?
L6-A-002.distractor_notes: Common error: b=12 (multiplied the constants), b=4 (only outer term)

L6-A-003.text: A sequence is: 7, 11, 15, 19, ... What is the nth term? The answer is 4n + c. What is c?
L6-A-003.explanation: Common difference = 4. nth term = 4n + c. When n=1: 4+c=7. c=3.
L6-A-003.distractor_notes: Common error: c=7 (just used first term), c=0 (forgot adjustment)

L6-A-004.distractor_notes: Common error: x=2 (subtracted 9-3 instead of adding)

L6-A-005.text: Expand and simplify: (x + 5)² . Answer is x² + bx + 25. What is b?
L6-A-005.distractor_notes: Most common error: x²+25 (forgetting middle term — the square shortcut mistake)

L7-A-002.text: Factorise: x² + 5x + 6. The factors are (x+2)(x+a). What is a?
L7-A-002.explanation: Need two numbers that multiply to 6 and add to 5: 2 and 3. So (x+2)(x+3). a=3.
L7-A-002.distractor_notes: Common error: a=6 (used product instead of factor pair)

L7-A-003.distractor_notes: Common error: x=12.25 (added fractions incorrectly as 7/12)

L7-A-004.text: A sequence has nth term 5n − 2. Which term has value 43?

L7-A-005.text: Factorise: x² − 9. The factors are (x + a)(x − a). What is a?
L7-A-005.explanation: Difference of two squares: x² − 9 = x² − 3² = (x+3)(x−3). a = 3.
L7-A-005.distractor_notes: Common error: a=9 (did not take square root)

L8-A-003.text: Solve: 3x + 2y = 16 and x + y = 7. What is y?
L8-A-003.distractor_notes: Common error: y=3 (found x not y), y=2 (arithmetic error)

L8-A-004.text: Solve x² − x − 6 = 0. What is the positive solution?
L8-A-004.inputHint: Type the positive value of x
L8-A-004.explanation: (x−3)(x+2)=0. x=3 or x=−2. Positive solution is 3.
L8-A-004.distractor_notes: Common error: x=2 (sign error in factorisation)

L8-A-005.text: Expand: (2x − 3)(x + 4). Answer is 2x² + bx − 12. What is b?
L8-A-005.distractor_notes: Common error: b=11 (forgot negative on −3x term)

L9-A-002.text: Use the quadratic formula on x² + 4x − 5 = 0. What is the positive solution?
L9-A-002.inputHint: Type the positive value of x
L9-A-002.explanation: a=1,b=4,c=−5. Discriminant=16+20=36. x=(−4±6)/2. x=1 or x=−5. Positive=1.
L9-A-002.distractor_notes: Common error: x=5 (forgot negative in formula)

L9-A-003.text: Solve: x² + y = 10 and y = x + 4. What is the positive value of x?
L9-A-003.inputHint: Type the positive value of x
L9-A-003.explanation: Substitute: x²+x+4=10. x²+x−6=0. (x+3)(x−2)=0. x=2 or x=−3. Positive=2.
L9-A-003.distractor_notes: Non-linear simultaneous — common error: treating as linear

L3-G-002.text: A rectangle is 8 cm long and 5 cm wide. What is the perimeter?
L3-G-002.distractor_notes: Common error: 40 (multiplied l×w, found area), 13 (added but forgot ×2)

L3-G-003.text: A regular hexagon has 6 equal sides. Each side is 4 cm. What is the perimeter?
L3-G-003.distractor_notes: Common error: 12 (multiplied by 3), 4+6=10 (added sides and number)

L4-G-002.text: A square has a perimeter of 36 cm. What is its area?
L4-G-002.distractor_notes: Common error: 36² = 1296 (used perimeter as side), 324 (forgot to square)

L4-G-003.text: An isosceles triangle has one angle of 100° at the top. What are the base angles?
L4-G-003.inputHint: Type each base angle in degrees
L4-G-003.explanation: Base angles are equal. 2 × base = 180 − 100 = 80. Each base angle = 40°.

L5-G-002.text: An L-shape: overall 10×8 cm with a 4×3 corner removed. What is the area?

L5-G-003.text: Two parallel lines cut by a transversal. One angle is 72°. What is the co-interior angle on the same side?

L5-G-004.text: A parallelogram has base 12 cm and perpendicular height 7 cm. What is the area?
L5-G-004.explanation: Area of parallelogram = base × height = 12 × 7 = 84 cm².
L5-G-004.distractor_notes: Common error: 42 (used ½ × base × height — triangle formula)

L6-G-002.text: A right triangle has hypotenuse 17 cm and one leg 8 cm. Find the other leg.

L6-G-003.text: Find the circumference of a circle with diameter 10 cm. Use π = 3.14.
L6-G-003.distractor_notes: Common error: 78.5 (used area formula), 15.7 (used radius not diameter)

L6-G-004.text: What is the sum of interior angles of a pentagon?

L6-G-005.text: A cuboid is 5 cm × 4 cm × 3 cm. What is the volume?
L6-G-005.distractor_notes: Common error: 94 (found surface area), 47 (found half surface area)

L7-G-002.text: Right triangle: adjacent = 8 cm, angle = 30°. Find the opposite side. Use sin30°=0.5, cos30°=0.866, tan30°=0.577.
L7-G-002.distractor_notes: Common error: 4 (used sin not tan), 9.24 (used hypotenuse formula). inputHint corrected to 2dp.

L7-G-003.text: A cylinder has radius 3 cm and height 8 cm. Find volume to 1dp. Use π=3.14.
L7-G-003.distractor_notes: Common error: 75.4 (used diameter not radius), 150.7 (halved result)

L7-G-004.text: Two similar triangles. Smaller has sides 3, 4, 5 cm. Larger has longest side 15 cm. What is the shortest side of the larger triangle?
L7-G-004.distractor_notes: Common error: 10 (added scale factor instead of multiplying)

L7-G-005.text: A cuboid is 6 cm × 4 cm × 3 cm. Find the space diagonal to 2dp.

L8-G-002.text: ABCD is a cyclic quadrilateral. Angle A = 75°. What is angle C?
L8-G-002.explanation: Opposite angles in a cyclic quadrilateral add to 180°. C = 180 − 75 = 105°.
L8-G-002.distractor_notes: Common error: 75 (thought they were equal), 285 (subtracted from 360)

L8-G-003.text: Right triangle: hypotenuse = 20 cm, angle = 35°. Find adjacent side. Use cos35° = 0.819.

L8-G-004.text: Volume of sphere with radius 6 cm. Use V = (4/3)πr³, π=3.14. Round to nearest whole number.

L9-G-002.text: Triangle: sides a=7, b=9, angle C=60°. Find side c using cosine rule. c²=a²+b²−2ab·cos(C). cos60°=0.5. What is c to 1dp?

L9-G-003.text: A tangent from point T touches a circle at A. OA = 6 cm (radius), OT = 10 cm. Find the length of the tangent TA.
L9-G-003.explanation: Tangent ⊥ radius. Right angle at A. TA = √(10²−6²) = √(100−36) = √64 = 8 cm.

L3-S-002.text: A bar chart shows: Red=8, Blue=5, Green=12, Yellow=3. How many more green than blue?

L3-S-003.explanation: Mode = most frequent value. 3 appears three times — more than any other.
L3-S-003.distractor_notes: Common error: 5 (thought mode was median), 9 (thought mode was highest)

L4-S-002.distractor_notes: Common error: students often pick 3 (unordered middle)

L4-S-003.text: A fair die is rolled. What is P(rolling a number greater than 4)? Type as a fraction numerator (denominator is 6).
L4-S-003.explanation: Numbers > 4 on a die: 5 and 6. P = 2/6 = 1/3. Numerator of 2/6 is 2.

L5-S-002.text: A bag has 3 red, 5 blue and 2 green balls. P(not red) as a decimal.

L5-S-003.text: The mean of 4 numbers is 9. Three of the numbers are 7, 11 and 6. What is the fourth?
L5-S-003.distractor_notes: Common error: 9 (thought fourth was also the mean)

L5-S-004.text: Flip a fair coin twice. What is P(two heads)? Type as a fraction numerator (denominator is 4).
L5-S-004.distractor_notes: Common error: 2 (counted H on each flip separately)

L6-S-002.text: A card is drawn from a standard 52-card deck. P(red OR King)? There are 26 red cards, 4 kings, 2 red kings. Type numerator (denominator is 52).
L6-S-002.distractor_notes: Common error: 30 (forgot to subtract the overlap)

L6-S-003.text: Grouped data: 0-10 (freq 3), 10-20 (freq 7), 20-30 (freq 5). What is the modal class? Type 1 for 0-10, 2 for 10-20, 3 for 20-30.
L6-S-003.distractor_notes: Common error: 3 (confused modal class with highest value)

L7-S-002.text: Bag: 5 red, 3 blue. Draw 2 with replacement. P(same colour both times)? Type as decimal to 2dp.
L7-S-002.distractor_notes: Answer-key fix applied: was 0.52, corrected to 0.53 to match explanation.

L7-S-003.text: Grouped data: 0-5 (freq 4, midpoint 2.5), 5-10 (freq 6, midpoint 7.5), 10-15 (freq 2, midpoint 12.5). Estimated mean to 1dp.

L7-S-004.text: Roll two dice. P(sum = 7)? Express as fraction. Type numerator (denominator is 36).

L8-S-002.text: Box: 6 white, 4 black. Draw 2 without replacement. P(one of each colour)? Type as decimal to 2dp.

L8-S-003.text: P(A) = 0.5, P(B|A) = 0.4, P(B|not A) = 0.2. Find P(B) using total probability.

L8-S-004.text: Three coins flipped. P(exactly 2 heads)? Type as fraction numerator (denominator is 8).
L8-S-004.distractor_notes: Common error: 4 (counted HHH as having 2 heads)

L9-S-002.text: P(A and B) = 0.12, P(B) = 0.4. Find P(A|B).

L9-S-003.text: Events A and B are mutually exclusive. P(A)=0.3, P(B)=0.45. Find P(A or B).
L9-S-003.explanation: Mutually exclusive: P(A or B) = P(A)+P(B) = 0.3+0.45 = 0.75.
L9-S-003.distractor_notes: Common error: 0.135 (multiplied — used AND formula instead of OR)
